title: phragmites australis subsp. pseudodonax
intro: Phragmites australis subsp. pseudodonax, also known as the Pseudodonax or Bitter Reed, is a subspecies of the common reed plant, native to Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating digestive issues and inflammation due to its anthraquinone and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Pseudodonax include reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, improving gut health, and alleviating pain. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal use, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions.
